item.title: The Bohemians who took over iconic Melbourne mansion Labassa
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Historic Melbourne mansion Labassa 'not just a building, it's a story'
item.description: From post-war Jewish refugees, who arrived with nothing but hope, to the bohemian artists and performers who took over in the 1970s, historic Melbourne mansion Labassa has more stories than rooms.

item.title: Alice Springs town camp renters sick of begging for 'bandaids', just want safe homes
item.description: Conditions in town camps in Alice Springs are under intense scrutiny, but the residents are caught in a tangled web of bureaucracy with little transparency on the money being spent and who exactly is responsible for what.

item.title: Watson's Tree Frog Breeding Program
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: The Drover's Wife becomes an opera in a new collaboration between Leah Purcell and George Palmer
item.description: Leah Purcell’s The Drover’s Wife is being transformed into an opera with music by George Palmer and starring soprano Nina Korbe.

item.title: Kira Puru gained fame as a musician, so why have they pivoted to acting in Bad Company?
item.description: Kira Puru is known for their triple j Hottest 100-charting energetic pop music, but this year the musician has roles in not one, but two of the biggest Australian TV shows. What's behind the pivot?

item.title: Home destroyed by fire rebuilt as an Earthship made of tyres, bottles and cans
item.description: Dee-Ann Kelly lost her home to fire in 2024, now she's rebuilding an Earthship, made from recycled materials in an effort to make her home more fire resilient.

item.title: Love story: Love when you don't know how long you've got
item.description: My 14-year-old daughter Pip has Williams syndrome. The future is uncertain and full of heartache, but my love for my baby is not.

item.title: Storm snap record losing run as Tigers coach laments 'negative smell'
item.description: The Melbourne Storm are winners for the first time since round two, snapping a club-record losing streak with a big win over the Wests Tigers.

item.title: Injury concern for women's world number one Aryna Sabalenka ahead of French Open
item.description: Women's tennis world number one Aryna Sabalenka has a fortnight to recover from injury before the French Open after her loss in Rome.

item.title: Woman charged after alleged antisemitic slur at children's netball game in Sydney
item.description: Jewish community leaders say a woman's alleged slur at an under-12 girls netball game in Sydney reflects a troubling pattern playing out on sporting fields.

item.title: Fashion designers are experimenting with AI, but not everyone is a fan
item.description: As Australian Fashion Week gets underway in Sydney, local and international designers debate the merits and detractions of using AI in the industry.

item.title: Split Enz: Tim Finn on the band’s history, legacy and reunion shows
item.description: Tim Finn on the past, the present and the possible future of Split Enz.

item.title: This week in history quiz, May 11: How much did Michael Jordan's signed Air Nike 1s sell for?
item.description: From the Tudor period right through to the 21st century, test your knowledge of the past with this week's history quiz.

item.title: Confined 17 exhibition showcases art of incarcerated First Nations women
item.description: A day after her 18th birthday, Flick Chafer-Smith hit rock bottom when she was sent to jail. She found salvation through art.

item.title: Authorities evacuate passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NSW Health agency to pay $50k in damages to worker in disability discrimination case
item.description: An admin officer has been awarded more than $50,000 in compensation after a tribunal found a NSW Health agency discriminated against her due to her disability.

item.title: Mary Fowler's Manchester City beats Sam Kerr's Chelsea in FA Cup semifinal, Dario Vidošić's Brighton beats Liverpool
item.description: Mary Fowler's goal kick-started Manchester City's FA Cup comeback to down Sam Kerr's Chelsea and set up a final against Brighton, led by former Socceroo Dario Vidošić.

item.title: This is what experts say you should not include in your resume
item.description: Here's what three experts recommend you don't put in your resume for the best chance of securing an interview or landing a role.

item.title: Knox Family brings country, rock and stories to Sydney Opera House for 'Generations and Dynasties'
item.description: Uncle Roger Knox, aka the Black Elvis, will perform a collection of his hit songs and a few yarns from his life at the Sydney Opera House, sharing the stage with his son and two grandsons.

item.title: Court to rule if Andrew Forrest will have to compensate the Yindjibarndi for mining their land
item.description: Tomorrow will mark a historic day for an outback WA community as the Federal Court decides how much compensation it is due after Andrew Forrest's FMG mined its land without permission.

item.title: Afghanistan's women's cricket team urges ICC to replicate FIFA recognition
item.description: Afghanistan's exiled women cricketers are urging the International Cricket Council (ICC) to follow FIFA's lead in recognising them as a legitimate national team.

item.title: Western Australia will fail to achieve net-zero by 2050 on current trajectory, Woodside-funded report warns
item.description: Woodside-commissioned modelling suggests WA is not on track to reach net zero by 2050, even if its massive Browse gas project proceeds, unless renewable energy is rolled out at unprecedented speed.

item.title: Sunwater CEO Glenn Stockton resigns amid Paradise Dam controversy
item.description: The ABC understands Sunwater CEO Glenn Stockton resigned on Friday after almost six years in the position.

item.title: First Nations man dies in custody at Lotus Glen Correctional Centre in Far North Queensland
item.description: The death is not being treated as suspicious and a report is being prepared for the coroner. Fourteen Indigenous people have died in Australian prisons this year.

item.title: Hasrat Gill stumbled upon the T20 World Cup on TV one day. It changed her life forever
item.description: State and WBBL cricketer Hasrat Gill has risen through the ranks of youth Australian cricket. She is one of many South Asian teenagers flocking to the sport.

item.title: Cafe-worthy breakfast and brunch recipes for relaxed mornings
item.description: From shakshuka to French toast, you can enjoy an indulgent breakfast or brunch at home with one of these recipes.

item.title: Aboriginal man expresses remorse after avoiding jail for vandalising culturally sensitive site
item.description: An Aboriginal man has pleaded guilty to vandalising a culturally significant site in Victoria's Alpine National Park.

item.title: Boy rescued after being trapped under train at North Melbourne Railway Station
item.description: A year 7 student is in a serious but stable condition in hospital after being hit by a train and becoming trapped for up to an hour.

item.title: Quadriplegic man invents way to pilot hands-free
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Hadley's shock return 10 weeks post-baby
item.description: Paige Hadley made a shock return to the Super Netball league less than three months after giving birth to her baby boy. Despite some commentary online questioning her priorities, the midcourter is more determined than ever to prove women can pursue multiple dreams.

item.title: The caring burden for single, childless women
item.description: Single women over 45 without children are much more likely to be spending time caring for family members who are aging, have a disability or chronic illness. And it's not just costing them financially.

item.title: How Mortal Kombat II brought Hollywood to the Gold Coast
item.description: The $110 million Mortal Kombat sequel is the next in a laundry list of productions putting Queensland on the radar for international films.

item.title: What will be in tonight's federal budget? Here are the major measures we know about so far
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers is unveiling his fifth budget tonight, and housing for younger Australians will be a centrepiece.

item.title: More or Less quiz: Guess where your money goes, and what it gets spent on
item.description: A new quiz format? In this economy? Celebrate budget day with our More or Less challenge.

item.title: Cricket Australia hopes Big Bash League privatisation could make it one of sport's 'grand slams'
item.description: Cricket Australia chief executive Todd Greenberg says private investment in the Big Bash League is "inevitable" and waiting for too long could mean "we've missed the boat" as more competitions pop up around the world.

item.title: Trump and Xi's Beijing summit must confront the AI cold war
item.description: When Trump last visited China in 2017, artificial intelligence was not yet the centre of global power. Now it is.

item.title: Eurovision begins amid boycotts and massive police operation in Vienna
item.description: This week, the Eurovision Song Contest will burst onto the stage in Vienna, and onto screens around the globe, but behind the colour and campness, this year's competition is shaping up as one of the most politically charged in history.

item.title: Study finds link between air pollution exposure and risk of ending up in hospital
item.description: A new study has found a clear link between air pollution exposure and the risk of ending up in hospital. It's a battle Julia Ovens knows only too well.

item.title: Queensland man pioneers app to fly drones using head movements
item.description: Ever since he was a young child, Christopher Hills has wanted to fly an aircraft. Now he has made that dream a reality.

item.title: Small robots used to help students consider careers in renewable energy
item.description: Students are using tiny, programmable robots to code in a bid to encourage them to consider careers in renewable energy due to predicted skill shortages.

item.title: Health minister invited to spend week with regional NDIS family
item.description: Regional families are anxiously waiting to see the impact of recently announced changes to the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), especially for those with autism.

item.title: AFL needs to do more to encourage Indigenous players as marked decline continues at top level
item.description: The AFL is considering a range of measures to boost the number of Indigenous players in the league following a significant decline in recent seasons.

item.title: Captain of Hantavirus-hit cruise ship praises crew and guests
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Multiple sclerosis patient spends night at Adelaide hospital waiting for an access taxi
item.description: A multiple sclerosis patient says he waited three hours for an access cab, only to be told in the middle of the night one would not be available, forcing him to spend the night at an Adelaide emergency room.

item.title: Murdoch University promises improvements after viral video shows issues with wheelchair access
item.description: The former Murdoch University student's video highlights the difficulty wheelchair users have navigating the campus since stair lifts were removed in 2023.

item.title: Blues On Broadbeach celebrates 25 years of music by the sea
item.description: As some festivals crumble, the Gold Coast's mayor says the 25-year-old Blues On Broadbeach event isn't going anywhere.

item.title: Travel quiz, May 13: Find your passport, it's time for a whirlwind world trip
item.description: Where was the world's first permanent commercial bungee jumping site set up? And which country is home to the historic city of Timbuktu?

item.title: Jayson Gillham on legal battle with Melbourne Symphony Orchestra and his tour Keys to Life
item.description: In 2024, Jayson Gillham's show was cancelled for his on-stage comments about Palestine. Now he brings a legal suit and a new recital tour with a Palestinian Jordanian artist.

item.title: Native Pacific black ducks at risk in Tasmania due to introduced species
item.description: The crossbreeding of Pacific black ducks with introduced species has devastated the native species in New Zealand — and conservationists say there's a risk that's happening in Tasmania too.

item.title: Federal budget disappointment for farmers and environmental groups
item.description: Weed and pest control programs have been cut in the federal budget as farmers and conservationists grapple with feral animals and noxious plants.

item.title: Sunrisers Hyderabad bowled out for 86 in IPL loss to Gujarat Titans
item.description: Australian Test cricketers Travis Head and Pat Cummins have been part of a heavy loss in the Indian Premier League.

item.title: Wil Anderson hates advertising. So why does he host a show about it?
item.description: It's been 18 years of hosting Gruen for Wil Anderson, but the self-confessed advertising cynic says the topic is more relevant than ever.

item.title: Southampton beats Middlesbrough 2-1 in extra time to reach EFL Championship playoff final
item.description: Southampton has defeated Middlesbrough to advance to the EFL Championship play-off final after a tense and heated two-leg semifinal.

item.title: Paratroopers jump to remote community to deliver medical support
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Eurovision semi finals kick off with musical nod to Austria-Australia confusion
item.description: The first semifinal of the Eurovision Song Contest includes a comedic musical interlude about the differences between Austria and Australia and sends 10 countries through to the grand final on Saturday.

item.title: Five-minute solar eclipse in WA's north to attract thousands to 'middle of nowhere'
item.description: One of the longest solar eclipses this century, albeit for five minutes, is set to put the remote East Kimberley on the world map.

item.title: Farrer's Indigenous and migrant communities worried by rise of One Nation
item.description: David Farley has won Farrer for One Nation in a landslide, but there are some in the community yet to be convinced of his ability to represent them.

item.title: Panthers confident of re-signing Nathan Cleary despite dad's departure
item.description: Penrith Panthers' four-time premiership-winning coach Ivan Cleary announces he will step aside as coach of the NRL club at the end of the 2027 season.

item.title: High Court sides with TasPorts over 2022 Mersey River tugboats sinking by Goliath
item.description: A shipping company's attempt to avoid paying millions of dollars to Tasmania's port authority over the sinking of two tugboats in Devonport has been overturned by Australia's High Court.

item.title: Free omega-3 testing to be introduced in SA for pregnant women in a bid to prevent preterm birth
item.description: The South Australian government has announced that pregnant women will be able to access free omega-3 blood tests from next month.

item.title: Four charts show Labor's budget is not just about the generation gap, but the wealth gap too
item.description: Just like it did in 2019, Labor is making an argument about wealth in Australia when it proposes negative gearing and capital gains tax changes.

item.title: Yindjibarndi urged to consider appeal against native title compensation decision
item.description: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to hand over the largest native title compensation payout in Australian history. But some say it falls painfully short of what is owed.

item.title: Why Ivan Cleary's decision to leave the Panthers is as unprecedented as their success
item.description: Ivan Cleary is doing what no other coach of his stature has ever done in Australian rugby league history, walking away from the NRL with his team at the height of their powers.

item.title: Veterans warn Invictus Australia budget cut could be life-threatening
item.description: The federal government is urged to reverse its decision to cut funding to Invictus Australia, an organisation that supports veterans.

item.title: Queensland banking on 'unbelievable' Tamika Upton to level State of Origin series
item.description: Queensland needs a win to keep its State of Origin hopes alive and is turning to the inspirational Tamika Upton to provide it.

item.title: What's in the 2026 budget for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people?
item.description: First Nations experts have their say on whether the $1.2 billion for Closing the Gap initiatives announced in this year's budget is likely to hit the mark.

item.title: Cannon by Lee Lai becomes first graphic novel to win the Stella Prize
item.description: Lee Lai's debut graphic novel, Stone Fruit, was longlisted for the Stella Prize in 2022. Four years on, she's won the prestigious award for her latest book, Cannon.

item.title: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage facing inquiry over $9m gift from crypto billionaire
item.description: Britain's parliamentary watchdog will begin an investigation into whether Reform UK leader Nigel Farage breached its rules by accepting a 5 million pound ($9.31 million) gift that he did not declare.

item.title: How Hollywood became a new home for classical music’s grandest ideas
item.description: From 19th-century concert traditions to Hollywood’s Golden Age, and back to the concert hall, movies are helping audiences experience live orchestral music.

item.title: It's the ABC More or Less quiz: Eurovision edition
item.description: We've put down our tiny flags and picked up our record books to challenge what you think you might know about Eurovision.

item.title: 'From Farage to Farrer': Rising populist tide makes Labor bolder
item.description: If this week's budget marked the start of the Albanese government's campaign for a third term in office, tonight's budget reply marks Angus Taylor's pitch to prevent a Liberal wipe-out.

item.title: Trump touches down in Beijing ahead of Xi meeting
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australians 'literally dying' as aged care average wait times surpass 365 days
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has conceded the state of aged care in Australia "is not good enough" after a new report revealed people are waiting an average of a year for services.

item.title: King Charles III opens UK parliament as PM Starmer expected to face leadership challenge
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer's future as Britain's prime minister remains on life support amid mounting speculation he could face a leadership challenge as early as Thursday, local time.

item.title: TV tower shutdown prompts concern about reliance on foreign satellites
item.description: Phone, internet and council-run TV services have been making way for foreign satellite technology in rural Queensland as ground infrastructure ages, forcing councils to shut down services.

item.title: Gun shop owners grapple with uncertainty amid firearm reform
item.description: A lack of clarity on the South Australian government's position on gun control reform has caused a downturn in revenue for retailers.

item.title: Surfside residents fear homes could be lost to sea in next storm
item.description: Residents living on an idyllic stretch of the NSW far south coast are demanding action to protect their homes from erosion.

item.title: New virus to control surging feral rabbit population at least 10 years away
item.description: Feral rabbits are causing damage on farms and in towns, but biological control research has been delayed by funding woes.

item.title: US Senate confirms Trump's pick Kevin Warsh to lead Federal Reserve
item.description: The US Senate has confirmed Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump's nominee to lead the Federal Reserve, bringing new leadership to the world's most powerful central bank at a fraught moment for the global economy.

item.title: Public memorial for 702 ABC Sydney presenter James Valentine to be held at Town Hall
item.description: Sydney Town Hall will host a memorial for the late radio presenter and musician, with limited spots available for members of the public.

item.title: Greenbushes residents say Talison's free car wash plan misses the mark
item.description: Residents plagued by dust from Australia's oldest and largest lithium mine say a plan for a free car wash by the mine's owner misses the mark.

item.title: UAE denies meeting with Netanyahu, Iran warns against neighbours 'colluding' with Israel - as it happened
item.description: Donald Trump is expected to ask for China's help to resolve the costly and unpopular conflict with Iran, but analysts say he is unlikely to get the support he wants.

item.title: Dennington Dogs forfeit remainder of men's senior Warrnambool District league season
item.description: A country Victorian football team that was beaten by a collective 1,101 points in four matches forfeits the remainder of the senior season.

item.title: How will the NT amend child protection laws? Will the Aboriginal Child Placement Principle change?
item.description: Here's what you need to know about the proposed changes to the NT child protection legislation, introduced into parliament on Wednesday.

item.title: Federal Court dismisses legal action over Murray flood plain restoration
item.description: A community group's argument that a delegate to the federal water minister made a legal error by approving the Nyah Floodplain Restoration Project has been rejected.

item.title: More than 800 northern corroboree frogs released into the wild in southern NSW to bolster numbers
item.description: More than 800 tiny, technicoloured northern corroboree frogs are released into the wild to try to bolster the numbers of one of Australia's most critically endangered amphibians.

item.title: Scottish Premier League title race goes to final day with Hearts leading Celtic by one point
item.description: Celtic's Kelechi Iheanacho scores a controversial penalty with the last kick of the game to secure a 3-2 win at Motherwell, sending the Scottish Premiership title race to a final-day showdown against leaders Heart of Midlothian.

item.title: Donald Trump tells Xi Jinping they will have 'fantastic future' in bilateral meeting — as it happened
item.description: The highly anticipated meeting saw Donald Trump meet his Chinese counterpart in Beijing for the first time in nearly nine years. Take a look back at the historic visit.

item.title: Newcastle Waters cattle station granted permit to cull 20,000 native birds
item.description: A permit to cull 20,000 native birds has been granted to a cattle station in the Northern Territory in response to an "overpopulation" of destructive and "exceptionally noisy" galahs and little corellas.

item.title: ISIS brides' arrest in Australia prompts anger in Syrian camp
item.description: The arrest of three Australian women linked to the Islamic State group sparks anger and frustration inside the Syrian refugee camp where they had lived for years.

item.title: Xi greets Trump in lavish welcoming ceremony in Beijing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick says Mark Opie will continue duties despite AFL probe
item.description: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick says team manager Mark Opie will continue his match-day duties despite being investigated by the AFL integrity unit over his links to a bookmaking firm.

item.title: Six passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship due to arrive in Perth today
item.description: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of a deadly hantavirus outbreak are testing negative for the illness and are symptom-free, the federal health minister says, as they prepare to be repatriated to Australia.

item.title: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick speaks to media in Darwin
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Roads of dead mice as plague blights standards of living in WA towns
item.description: As locals in a Western Australian town battle a siege of mice, farmers still do not have permission to use a high-strength bait to combat them.

item.title: Madonna, Shakira and BTS to co-headline World Cup final half-time show
item.description: The first-ever FIFA World Cup final half-time show has been announced.

item.title: An Accidental Leader | Rob Armstrong
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Bosisto's to triple its eucalyptus oil production with new $10 million distillery in central Victoria
item.description: A 174-year-old Australian company is trying to address a decline in local eucalyptus oil production by opening a new distillery in central Victoria.

item.title: Calls for more education following the suspected death of Mornington Peninsula's beloved 'Sammy' the seal
item.description: Marine experts are calling for greater education on aquatic life following the death of a seal believed to be known by Mornington Peninsula locals as Sammy.

item.title: Miraculous try-savers hand NSW second straight Origin series win
item.description: For the second straight year, NSW secures the Women's State of Origin shield in Game II, with Jesse Southwell and Teagan Berry coming up with stunning try-savers to get the Blues over the line.

item.title: Mannagh's heavenly night leads Cats to statement win over Lions
item.description: Geelong records a 41-point win over Brisbane on the back of Shaun Mannagh's five-goal, 30-disposal performance at the Gabba.

item.title: Leadership hopefuls Wes Streeting and Andy Burnham edge towards challenging UK PM Keir Starmer
item.description: The UK's health secretary has resigned from cabinet and another Labour MP has quit parliament paving the way for a potential leadership challenge to Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

item.title: ICC fugitive Ronald dela Rosa's whereabouts unknown after fleeing Philippine Senate
item.description: Senator Ronald dela Rosa, who is wanted by the International Criminal Court on charges of crimes against humanity, had been holed up inside the Philippine Senate since Monday to avoid arrest. His whereabouts is now unknown.

item.title: Latvian PM Evika Silina resigns in row over stray Ukrainian drones
item.description: Drones launched by Ukraine that were jammed by Russia either flew over or crashed in the Baltic country's territory, with local residents critical of the government's response.

item.title: Seven dead, scores missing in Kyiv after Russian drone hits apartment building
item.description: Russia launches hundreds of drones at Ukraine in the third straight day of widespread attacks on targets across the country.

item.title: Chinese President Xi Jinping hosts state banquet for US President Donald Trump
item.description: Guests dined on Beijing roast duck and tiramisu while the People's Liberation Army band performed some classic hits.

item.title: Sam Kerr to leave Chelsea Football Club after six years
item.description: The Matildas star has spent over six years playing for the London-based team.

item.title: Sensory-friendly show gives theatre lovers a more inclusive experience
item.description: The State Theatre Company South Australia has introduced a sensory-friendly performance for a broader range of audience members including neurodivergent people.

item.title: Ambulance Victoria data shows 59 overdose call-outs at Crown Melbourne in two years
item.description: Paramedics have attended more than 50 suspected overdoses at Crown Melbourne in the past two years, data released exclusively to the ABC shows.

item.title: TasInsure unravelling a lesson for Tasmanian Liberals in promise management
item.description: In a week where "broken promise" is being shouted by the Tasmanian Liberals in support of their federal colleagues' attack on the Labor budget, closer to home, a promise of their own is giving them grief.

item.title: Modernising Queensland veterinary act essential for future of industry, experts say
item.description: Experts says veterinary technologists and nurses are being under-utilised in a sector they say is in "crisis."

item.title: Magic Round crowds told to pack raincoats ahead of patchy Queensland showers
item.description: A trough in the west and onshore winds in the east will bring showers to parts of Queensland, however the driest parts are set to miss out.

item.title: Liberal Party mistakenly emails branch about 'deceased member'
item.description: When John Ternel quit the Victorian Liberal Party this week the party mistakenly sent an email to members saying he had died. Mr Ternel says the ordeal confirmed to him that the Victorian Liberals are in shambles.

item.title: ABC weekly news quiz: A Eurovision boycott, a pungent frog, a mysterious red globule and some impressive Neanderthals
item.description: This is not the week to boycott the news quiz, when a particularly ripe frog and a big, red … something … await you.

item.title: Australian government rejects clean-up deadline for Antarctica's Wilkes Station
item.description: The Australian government has officially ruled out cleaning up Antarctica's contaminated Wilkes Station before the end of the decade, as previously recommended by a federal parliamentary committee.

item.title: South East SA locals voice outrage at proposal to lift fracking ban
item.description: Producers on South Australia's Limestone Coast say they are stunned by a government proposal to lift the fracking ban in the region.

item.title: Residents feel impact of no permanent GP in Strahan since 2017
item.description: Jamie hopes his small town's almost decade-long run without a permanent GP is about to end, so he will not have to keep repeating his story to every doctor who passes through.

item.title: Archaeologists work to restore colonial-era boat found during construction of Barangaroo metro
item.description: Maritime archaeologists are working to restore the oldest colonial-era boat ever found in Australia, discovered during the construction of the Barangaroo metro station in 2018.

item.title: Archaeologists restore colonial-era boat found in Barangaroo
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Jensen Plowright avoids late crash to finish fourth on Giro d'Italia stage six, Davide Ballerini claims win
item.description: Jensen Plowright never expected to be contesting for stage victories at this year's Giro d'Italia. But the withdrawal of Kaden Groves and yet another calamitous crash gave him his first chance on stage six.

item.title: Seismic testing linked by research to decreased fish catch numbers in Gippsland
item.description: New research links seismic testing used for oil and gas exploration with decreases in commercial fish catch rates in Victoria's east.

item.title: Music program takes regional artists from the Northern Territory to UK for The Great Escape festival
item.description: Northern Territory musicians attending The Great Escape music festival in the UK are hoping their experience will open more doors for regional artists.

item.title: Delta Goodrem makes Eurovision 2026 grand final after performing pop ballad Eclipse
item.description: Delta Goodrem will perform in the Eurovision 2026 grand final for Australia after a rousing performance of the song Eclipse during the semifinal.

item.title: Delta Goodrem performs at Eurovision semifinals
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Mount Isa homes to be sold after Indigenous organisation fails to pay rates
item.description: Tenants in some Mount Isa homes have endured years without repairs, hot water or proper leases and now one corporation's failures could leave them homeless.

item.title: Seals found entangled in fishing line or rope on the rise
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wildlife rescuers say more fur seals are becoming entangled in fishing gear
item.description: Data from the wildlife rescue group ORRCA shows an increase in the number of fur seal entanglements as they seek out fishing lines in attempts to find an easy meal.

item.title: Min Woo Lee takes share of clubhouse lead at PGA Championship, Scotty Scheffler ominously poised
item.description: Australian Min Woo Lee enjoys a share of the first-round clubhouse lead, but Masters champ Rory McIlroy has endured a rough start to the PGA Championship.

item.title: Man faces court charged with murdering elderly mother in her Canberra home
item.description: Marcus Kauffman made a brief appearance in the ACT Magistrates Court this morning, where no bail application was made.

item.title: WA Premier Roger Cook backs federal budget housing focus
item.description: Roger Cook welcomes the federal budget's focus on housing, but he's less than thrilled about NDIS changes and the funding of infrastructure and aged care.

item.title: Melanie Lynskey pays tribute to the women driving the Pike River Mine disaster campaign
item.description: Standing side by side with real-life heroes Anna Osborne and Sonya Rockhouse, the Yellow Jackets star is a passionate ally for their cause.

item.title: Victorian country firefighters slam bureaucracy for slashing roadside burns by 90pc
item.description: Victorian fire authorities release new findings that show how planned burns helped control and contain the devastating summer bushfires.

item.title: Vaccine program almost halves number of young babies hospitalised with RSV
item.description: A new immunisation program for pregnant mothers and newborns has almost halved the number of young babies being hospitalised with a serious respiratory virus, according to a new report.

item.title: Manjimup's migrant community unite to mourn deaths of Vanuatu farm workers in horror crash
item.description: Residents of a WA country town have come together to mourn the deaths of two Pacific island farm workers as investigations into their deaths continue.

item.title: Australia vs Ireland AFLW clash locked in for North Sydney Oval in August
item.description: Australia and Ireland will meet in an international women's match under Australian rules to kick off the 2026 AFLW season.

item.title: National Commissioner for Indigenous children says people chosen to lead NT child protection review won't 'cut it'
item.description: The National Commissioner for Indigenous children has criticised the selection of a retired NSW police commissioner and long-standing NT public servant as the leaders of a review into the territory's child protection system.

item.title: 'We want the straits open': Trump says China agrees on ending the Iran war — as it happened
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping praised the building of a "stable relationship" with the US as the two world leaders met in Beijing. Take a look back at the historic state visit. Take a look back at how the day unfolded.

item.title: Northern Territory records Australia's first diphtheria death reported in almost a decade amid outbreak
item.description: The Northern Territory has recorded the country's first reported death from diphtheria in almost a decade, as experts urge people to get vaccinated.

item.title: Calls for drought declaration as southern Queensland graziers begin to destock
item.description: Graziers start destocking to preserve dwindling feed with rainfall well below average across the region.

item.title: Federal Court rejects northern cattle industry's appeal over impact of live export ban
item.description: Australia's northern cattle industry says it is "deeply disappointed" by a Federal Court decision to dismiss its appeal over compensation for the 2011 live export ban.

item.title: Carlton interim coach Josh Fraser not ready to take full-time role after Michael Voss exit
item.description: Josh Fraser will lead Carlton for the remainder of the season after Michael Voss's exit, but isn't prepared to do the job full-time just yet.

item.title: The federal government wants to use AI to speed up drug and housing approvals. Can we trust it?
item.description: This week’s budget revealed a number of ways the federal government is expected to use artificial intelligence to cut back on red tape. One expert says the benefits are "mostly good".

item.title: Eight people arrested at North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site, police say
item.description: Police say eight people have been arrested at the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site this morning.

item.title: Push to overturn South East fracking ban to be blocked by One Nation, Liberals
item.description: A controversial plan to end a ban on fracking in South Australia's South East two years earlier than planned, appears to be all but dead, with the Liberals and One Nation vowing to oppose legislation.

item.title: Through my eyes: Aiden Hinson
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers get another two weeks pay reprieve
item.description: Workers at Australia's only manganese smelter will be thrown an additional lifeline as the state and federal governments step in with a two-week pay offer. It comes days before a previous short-term deal was due to expire.

item.title: Xi made a historical reference as a warning to Trump. What is the Thucydides Trap?
item.description: The US and China have been rivals for almost 80 years, but when the countries' leaders met in Beijing on Thursday, Xi Jinping made reference to a far more ancient feud.

item.title: Xi Jinping and Donald Trump deliver remarks on the second and final day of talks
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Four students in hospital after suspected drug 'overdose' at Central Coast high school
item.description: Fifteen students at a Central Coast high school have been assessed by paramedics, with four being taken to hospital.

item.title: Former Victorian premier Daniel Andrews to be immortalised with statue
item.description: The government says work is already underway to honour the state's longest-serving Labor premier.

item.title: Could Delta Goodrem win Eurovision for Australia? The odds have soared in her favour
item.description: In a dazzling show of light and a powerful, tone-perfect delivery, Delta Goodrem may have just given Australia a real shot at winning Eurovision for the first time.

item.title: Socceroos star Mo Toure says he went with a hunch by moving to Norwich City
item.description: Rising Socceroos striker Mo Toure says a "gut feeling" was the reason behind his wildly successful move to the English Championship.

item.title: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping meet at Zhongnanhai Garden
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Donald Trump boards Air Force One after Beijing trip
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Chloroform suspected as reason Launceston General Hospital ward shut, staff ill, documents reveal
item.description: An incident that prompted a Tasmanian hospital theatre to evacuate, and left several staff feeling ill, was possibly caused by chloroform, documents reveal.

item.title: Costs under 'pressure' for controversial Perth Park racetrack project, state treasurer says
item.description: Despite assurances to the contrary, WA Treasurer Rita Saffioti has conceded it would be difficult for the government to stick to the $217.5 million budget for the Perth Park project.

item.title: Invasive 'barbecue stopper' Asian tiger mosquito detected away from entry ports on mainland for first time
item.description: The world's most invasive mosquito has previously been found at borders, islands, and ports, but has just been discovered in a remote mainland Queensland community.

item.title: Five Italians die following cave diving incident in the Maldives
item.description: The group was exploring a series of underwater caves at a depth of about 50 metres in Vaavu Atoll in the Maldives.

item.title: Auckland FC cruises past Adelaide United to reach maiden A-League Men's grand final
item.description: Auckland becomes the first team from New Zealand to qualify for an A-League Men's season decider after making light work of Adelaide at Hindmarsh Stadium.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of patron saints, the Friends finale and a stone cold 90s club banger
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Neo-Nazis fundraising to challenge laws that listed them as hate group
item.description: The leader of Neo-Nazi group National Socialist Network, Thomas Sewell, has appeared in an interview online alongside notorious US Neo-Nazi James Mason, who is listed as a terrorist entity by the Canadian government.

item.title: Most widespread May rain in a decade to reach all Australian states
item.description: The most widespread May rain in a decade is about to sweep across Australia as the first north-west cloudband of 2026 becomes a reality.

item.title: Sam Coulton turned his family's cotton crop into a clothing brand now stocked across Australia
item.description: Queensland cotton farmer and entrepreneur Sam Coulton has spent decades diversifying beyond agriculture, building a fashion brand and tourism business in Goondiwindi while creating jobs and investing in the future of his rural town.

item.title: Bronze bust of Scott Morrison joins Prime Ministers Avenue in Ballarat
item.description: A thinner ex-PM cracks jokes as his bust is revealed, making him the 30th Australian leader with a likeness in Ballarat's Prime Ministers Avenue.

item.title: Natural burials begin at NSW conservation cemetery Walawaani Way
item.description: After nearly 10 years getting public health and council approvals, a natural burial ground on the NSW south coast begins letting people have a simpler, more environmentally friendly farewell.

item.title: How WA's once-revered donkeys became 'nuisance' pests and canned meat
item.description: Before roads and vehicles, teams of donkeys used to haul huge loads across remote parts of Western Australia. But the beloved animal became a declared pest that even the meat trade couldn't save.

item.title: Migrant nurses training to fill skills gaps in rural SA health project
item.description: The rural town of Cleve in SA is getting a much-needed boost to its health system with a pilot program sponsoring five nurses from the Philippines to undertake traineeships to become enrolled nurses.

item.title: Counting underway in by-election in Brisbane seat of Stafford, swing towards LNP
item.description: Labor candidate Luke Richmond wants to make the contest a referendum on health, while LNP candidate Fiona Hammond is focused on fuel, the cost of living, and crime.

item.title: Fracking ban rethink forced onto back-burner — but is it delaying the inevitable?
item.description: The SA government argues South East fracking would help unlock gas reserves at a time of limited supply — but it's a move that's vehemently opposed by many in the local community.

item.title: Vietnam War Battle of Coral–Balmoral memorial unveiled in Gundagai
item.description: The longest and deadliest battle for Australian troops in the Vietnam War has finally been memorialised with a statue to the Battle of Coral–Balmoral erected in Gundagai.

item.title: Mature Artists Dance Experience for over 50s hopes for increased arts funding ahead of Tasmanian budget
item.description: As the social and health benefits of a not-for-profit dance group for over 50s are felt, industry insiders hope more funding will be invested in the arts sector ahead of the Tasmanian state budget.

item.title: Aunty Polly Cutmore shares her family's stories of survival at Dhirri-aay-aay
item.description: Back on country, Aunty Polly Cutmore says she feels like a child again when visiting her ancestral lands. Now, as a respected Goombri elder, she shares the stories of her country.

item.title: This Place: Terry Hie Hie
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tiger shark beheading in Albany prompts call for greater regulation
item.description: A researcher speaks out against the fishing of sharks after footage of a huge tiger shark being caught and decapitated circulated on social media.

item.title: First Nations father teaches dying Indigenous language to sons through bedtime story
item.description: Five years ago, Gudjala man Braithen Knox began learning his mob's language, which has fewer than 10 speakers. Now a father of two, he is passing it on to his sons through bedtime stories.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship Australians isolating in Perth unlikely to be paid out, experts say
item.description: But the lawyer who led the class action in the COVID-19 era Ruby Princess case has warned the cruise company will likely argue Australian law had no jurisdiction over the matter.

item.title: Passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrive in Perth
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Residents fear Oyster Bay shallowing out and becoming mudflat
item.description: A bin that has been sticking out of the mud for 12 years has become a symbol for an ongoing issue of sediment build-up in Oyster Bay in Sydney's Sutherland Shire.

item.title: Bill to support environmental water flows an 'attack' on property rights
item.description: Politicians and landholders vow to fight a controversial new New South Wales law that will allow the inundation of private property from environmental water flows under the Murray-Darling Basin Plan.

item.title: Man stabbed in his sleep and left in serious condition in Tennant Creek, NT police say
item.description: A 23-year-old man has been arrested over the alleged stabbing of another man who was sleeping in a home in remote Tennant Creek about 990 kilometres south-east of Darwin.

item.title: Origin hopefuls stand up as Cowboys coach hails 'gritty' win
item.description: Todd Payten said his side's victory over the Roosters was "as good a win as I've had" with the club as Melbourne notched back-to-back wins after a messy 34-8 victory over Parramatta and the Sea Eagles thrash Wests Tigers in Magic Round at Lang Park.

item.title: Labor's Luke Richmond on track to win by-election in Brisbane seat of Stafford
item.description: At the end of counting on Saturday night, there has been a 4.1 per cent swing against Labor after preferences, with additional postal votes still to be counted.

item.title: Sydney FC defeats Newcastle Jets on penalties to progress to A-League Men grand final
item.description: Eli Adams's 96th-minute equaliser proves to be in vain as Sydney FC downs Newcastle on penalties to progress to the A-League Men season decider.

item.title: Eight dead and 35 injured after train collides with public bus in Bangkok
item.description: Footage of the incident shows a group of vehicles queuing across a railway line, before a cargo train suddenly ploughs into the side of the bus and pushes it into oncoming traffic.

item.title: Rochelle Jordan on Through The Wall, critical acclaim and learning to love her own music
item.description: The Canadian singer won widespread acclaim with her third album. Now she brings it to Australia.

item.title: Khaled Sabsabi's new installations 'didn't have a home'. Now they're at the Venice Biennale
item.description: Last year, Khaled Sabsabi was appointed, sacked and then reinstated as Australia's representative to the Venice Biennale. Now he opens two installations at the world's most prestigious art exhibition.

item.title: Can you score top marks on Guy Montgomery's spelling quiz?
item.description: Are you ready to test your spelling skills like your little lunch depends on it? Come on in.

item.title: The spiral-shaped trap: AI chatbots and the descent into delusion
item.description: Rodrigues thought an AI chatbot could help him escape poverty and build wealth. Instead, he became trapped in a spiral of delusion and dependence.

item.title: Victorian farmer Rob Armstrong has been a volunteer firefighter for 40 years.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Accidental leader' of CFA levy protest Rob Armstrong reveals scars left by childhood bushfire trauma
item.description: Every time farmer and volunteer firefighter Rob Armstrong gets a call to battle a bushfire, something inside says not to do it. But he goes anyway. He knows what it’s like to be trapped in the flames and rescued by volunteers.

item.title: Donald Trump discusses a renovation project at Washington DC's reflecting pool
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Three reasons Donald Trump's plans for Washington's reflecting pool are causing controversy
item.description: Donald Trump has chosen a new colour for Washington DC's Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. Here are three reasons why it's proving to be controversial.

item.title: Aspiration has changed since the Howard era. This budget is finally catching up
item.description: At the turn of the century, "aspiration" described Australians who wanted to put their kids into private school and university, and to build their wealth by getting an investment property or two. Today, it means something humbler; millions of younger Australians simply aspire to own a home one day, to have some shelter.

item.title: Why some people pay more for Islamic financing to avoid conventional home loans
item.description: When Wildan decided to buy a property he didn't even consider a conventional loan. Instead, he opted for a niche option that caters to a growing segment of Australia's population.

item.title: Malnourished and overtrained, Olympic gymnast didn't have energy for a period
item.description: Alexandra Kiroi-Bogatyreva trained up to 10 hours a day, six days a week, often without the proper nutrition. She wants others to know what that can do to the body.

item.title: People with disability spill the tea on love, intimacy, and desire with comedian Madeleine Stewart.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Spilling the tea on love, lust and everything in between
item.description: It's a stormy February night in Adelaide, and Jacqueline Tedmanson is at the hospital with her partner Jackson, waiting to meet their first baby.

item.title: Rewiring Australia campaign for renters to be included in electrical future
item.description: A standard power plug for charging your home from an EV is among the measures proposed to improve energy standards for rental properties.

item.title: Sam Kerr ties Chelsea's goals record in final Women's Super League match
item.description: Sam Kerr ties the record for the most Women's Super League goals for Chelsea as she plays her final match with the London club after more than six years.
